findings.evidence: `Prod.lean:47` has `@[expose] public section`; `Prod.lean:214` defines `noncomputable def twistedProd`; `Prod.lean:242` supplies `@[simp] lemma twistedProd_apply`.
findings.fix: Mark `twistedProd` `@[no_expose]` or move it out of the exposed section; keep `twistedProd_apply` as the public normal form.
findings.issue: `twistedProd` is defined under the file's `@[expose] public section`, exposing the representation of the bundled form even though `twistedProd_apply` is the intended characterization.

findings.evidence: `SymplecticTransport.lean:45` has `@[expose] public section`; `SymplecticTransport.lean:115` defines `def IsSymplectomorphism`; `SymplecticTransport.lean:121` and `:132` give the characteristic iff lemmas.
findings.fix: Mark `IsSymplectomorphism` `@[no_expose]` or move it to a non-exposed public section; consumers should use `isSymplectomorphism_iff`, `.apply`, and `isSymplectomorphism_iff_transport_eq`.
findings.issue: `IsSymplectomorphism` is also added under an exposed section, making the predicate body part of the public reduction surface despite having pointwise and transport characterizations.

summary: The characteristic lemmas are mostly present, but two new public definitions are placed inside exposed sections even though their users have non-unfolding API. Hide those bodies and keep downstream use through the supplied apply/iff lemmas.
